input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the powertrain on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the powertrain on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. The scattershield is made from steel. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.5.2.7a specified in the FSAE rule document? To answer the question, use the scale bar shown at the top of the engineering drawing to compute necessary dimensions in the drawing.  First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.5.2.7
            TITLE: chain drive - scatter shields for chains must:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.7: Chain Drive - Scatter shields for chains must:
T.5.2.7 Chain Drive - Scatter shields for chains must: 
a. Be made of 2.66 mm (0.105 inch) minimum thickness steel (no alternatives are allowed)
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 67 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
b. Have a minimum width equal to three times the width of the chain 
c. Be centered on the center line of the chain  
d. Stay aligned with the chain under all conditions

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule T.5.2.7a mandates that scatter shields for chains must be made of 2.66 mm minimum thickness steel. While the prompt states that the scattershield is made from steel, the engineering drawing and associated dimensions do not provide a measurable dimension for the thickness of the scatter shield. Therefore, compliance with the critical minimum thickness requirement of 2.66 mm cannot be verified using the provided technical data.

Answer: No
